Class UserView

java.lang.Object
com.vaadin.flow.component.Component
com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.ui.security.UserView
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.vaadin.flow.component.AttachNotifier, com.vaadin.flow.component.ClickNotifier<com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout>, com.vaadin.flow.component.DetachNotifier, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasElement, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasSize, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasStyle, com.vaadin.flow.component.HasTheme, com.vaadin.flow.component.shared.HasThemeVariant<com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayoutVariant>, Serializable
@Route("users") @PermitAll public class UserView extends com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout
Master-detail CRUD screen for PUser. The grid lists all active users (UserService.getAllUsers() already filters out deactivated ones), the side form creates a new user or edits the selected one. There is NO hard delete: the "Deactivate" action soft-deletes via UserService.deactivateUser(Long) (which refuses to deactivate an admin), after which the user drops out of the active list.

Same conventions as CompanyView/DepartmentView (master-detail SplitLayout with a draggable divider, placeholder when nothing is selected). Two view-specific choices driven by the service:

  • Update goes through UserService.partialUpdateUser(Long, PUser) — it loads the managed entity and copies only the supplied scalar fields (blank password = unchanged), so no detached entity is round-tripped and no lazy relation (permissions/department) is touched. Username and the admin flag are intentionally not editable here.
  • Roles are assigned via a MultiSelectComboBox and persisted through the dedicated UserService.setRoles(Long, java.util.Set) (a full replace) — partialUpdateUser/createUser deliberately never touch roles. The lazy roles collection is never read off the detached grid entity; the currently assigned ids come from UserService.getRoleIds(Long) and pre-select the selector items.
  • Create builds a fresh user via the Lombok builder with active(true) — a plain new PUser() would leave the @Builder.Default fields at their raw defaults (active=false, null collections), which would immediately hide the new user from the active list.
The password is required when creating and optional when editing (left blank keeps the current one).
Author:
peter haller
See Also:
